title: Supplies / Laptop Denial
desc: VA counselor verbally denied a computer or claimed a flat annual spending cap.

fields.name: schoolRequired
fields.label: Does the school require a computer for all students in this program?

fields.name: disabilityNeed
fields.label: Is a computer package necessary to accommodate your disability limitations?

fields.name: verbalDenialReason
fields.label: What reason did the counselor give for the verbal denial?
fields.placeholder: e.g. Citing a standard $500 spending limit

errors: VA counselor verbally denying necessary supplies instead of providing a written decision notice (VA Form 20-0998).

Citing Post-9/11 GI Bill cap rules ($1,000/yr) or a flat $500 VR&E limit — Chapter 31 supplies are not subject to the Chapter 33 book stipend cap, but still require VA authorization based on rehabilitation necessity under 38 C.F.R. § 21.212.

evidenceChecklist.text: Syllabus explicitly requiring a personal computer or specialized tools
School policy letter stating laptop/tech is required for all students
Personal statement explaining technology need due to disability or disadvantages
OT report or doctor recommendation for adaptive hardware/software

bestNextActionRules.action: Submit supplies request under 38 C.F.R. § 21.212 showing necessity.

reviewLaneQuestions.question: Does your course syllabus explicitly state a computer or laptop is required?
Did the counselor cite an office budget cap (e.g., $500 or $1,000) for supplies?
